top_entities.name: Amazon
top_entities.news_summary: Worker death at PDX9 (Troutdale, Oregon) warehouse on April 6, 2026 — a 46-year-old tote runner collapsed and died on the warehouse floor. Multiple employees report management ordered them to continue loading trucks for over an hour as the body remained on the floor, with supervisors telling workers to 'turn around and not look.' Oregon OSHA says its 'non-work related' classification applies only to federal reporting requirements, not to whether the workplace contributed to the death. Workers cite recently installed soundproof curtains that reduced airflow in an already heat-intensive facility. The PDX9 facility had the worst injury rate of 23 major Amazon centers in 2018 (26% of workers injured). Coverage broke April 13-16 across TechCrunch, NewsNation, Novara Media, Yahoo Finance, and Moneywise. This is a major new development post-dating yesterday's NLRB/labor scan. Composite 21.6 — within 3 points of the critical/developing band boundary at 20.

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.news_summary: The 2025–2026 Iranian protests and massacres represent one of the largest humanitarian atrocity events of 2026 that this benchmark has not yet fully assessed. The uprising began December 28, 2025; security forces used live fire on protesters beginning January 8, 2026. Confirmed death toll: HRANA documented 7,015 deaths including at least 6,508 protesters as of February 5, 2026; the NY Times called it the greatest massacre in Iran 'in many decades.' At least 10,000+ arrested. A second wave of student-led protests erupted February 21 at university campuses. As of April 11, 2026, rallies protest executions of 6 political prisoners affiliated with the PMOI/MEK plus protesters from the January uprising. EU sanctions extended April 13, 2026 (Iran sanctions regime last extended until April 13). UN Human Rights Council resolution (January 2026) extended fact-finding mandate. EU added 16 new persons/entities in March 2026. UN Special Rapporteur states the evidence justifies investigating possible crimes against humanity. Current composite 2.8 (critical band) — last assessed April 16, pre-dating the April 11-14 rally coverage and EU sanctions expiry/continuation.

top_entities.name: Ford Motor
top_entities.news_summary: Ford issued a major NHTSA-triggered safety recall on April 14, 2026, affecting 1,392,935 F-150 trucks (2015–2017 model years with 6R80 transmission). Root cause: degraded electrical connections in the transmission lead frame cause the PCM to receive incorrect signals from the Transmission Range Sensor, triggering unintended downshifts — in worst cases, a sudden drop from 6th gear to 2nd gear at highway speeds. Two injuries and one accident are already confirmed. NHTSA flagged the issue in October 2024; Ford's internal review opened immediately but the recall was only formally submitted April 14, 2026. Owner notification due July 13-17, 2026. A separate April 2026 Ram recall (65,348 vehicles) and Tesla seat fastener recall also active this week, but the Ford incident is the largest single safety recall in the Fortune 500 space this week. Composite 48.4 (functional band) — never assessed.

top_entities.name: South Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: South Sudan is experiencing escalating violence at a scale not seen since the 2018 Revitalised Peace Agreement. OHCHR issued two emergency statements in April 2026: (1) April 2 — UN experts urge immediate protection of civilians amid escalating crisis (267,000+ displaced in Jonglei state alone in 2026); (2) April 9 — escalating violence has created a child trafficking crisis. Fighting between SSPDF and SPLA-IO continues across multiple states with widespread conflict-related sexual violence and massive displacement. UN Secretary-General issued a press statement expressing deep concern over heightened violence and community targeting. The UN Security Council is expected to vote on renewing the UNMISS mandate before April 30 expiry. Never assessed. Composite 0 (critical band).

top_entities.name: Ukraine
top_entities.news_summary: Ukraine-Russia Orthodox Easter ceasefire (32 hours from April 11, 4pm) collapsed quickly, with both sides accusing the other of violations: Russia claimed 1,971 Ukrainian violations including 258 artillery strikes, 1,329 FPV drone strikes, and 375 munition drops; Ukraine disputed these figures. The Kremlin stated it would not extend the truce unless Kyiv accepts Russia's territorial demands (all of Donetsk) — demands Kyiv called unacceptable. UN General Assembly adopted a resolution demanding immediate unconditional ceasefire on April 12 (the fourth anniversary of the war). US-facilitated peace talks remain stalled. France and UK maintain plans for military hubs. The Easter ceasefire collapse is significant new evidence of the durability of hostilities and continued civilian harm. Composite 50 (functional band) — last assessed April 16.

top_entities.name: Russia
top_entities.news_summary: Three significant April 2026 developments: (1) Russian Supreme Court designated International Memorial as an 'extremist' organization on April 9, 2026, banning all activities under threat of imprisonment — Memorial maintained the most comprehensive database of political prisoners in Russia. (2) April 15 report: 15-year-old Arseny Turbin (now 17), one of Russia's youngest political prisoners, faces new criminal charges that could extend his sentence well beyond his existing 5-year terrorism conviction. (3) Political prisoner count has risen from 805 at end of 2024 to 1,217 (108 women) as of April 2026, with over 100 teenagers facing prosecution. Memorial's total list exceeds 3,000 political prisoners. Russia composite 0 (critical band) — never assessed.

top_entities.name: UnitedHealth Group
top_entities.news_summary: UnitedHealth Group is set to report Q1 2026 earnings on April 21 — a watershed moment for the DOJ criminal Medicare fraud investigation and the 'one-year-after-CEO-shooting' accountability reckoning. Key facts: (1) DOJ criminal probe into Medicare Advantage billing (inflated risk scores, diagnoses added without physician confirmation) is active and UHG confirmed in July 2025 it is complying with formal criminal and civil requests. (2) UHG earnings fell 41% in full-year 2025, with a $2.88B Q4 charge including $799M in cyberattack remediation. (3) The insurer is exiting 2.3-2.8 million members from unprofitable Medicare Advantage contracts. (4) Prior authorization reforms promised post-CEO shooting are narrowly implemented. Composite 16.9 (critical band) — last assessed April 16. The Q1 2026 earnings call April 21 will be the first since Stephen Hemsley's return as CEO and may include new DOJ disclosures. Volatility: 3.1 points from the critical/developing boundary at 20.

top_entities.name: OpenAI
top_entities.news_summary: Carrying forward from yesterday's top-priority assessment: the Illinois SB 3444 AI liability shield bill, which OpenAI actively backed, has a committee deadline of April 24, 2026. Senator Cunningham acknowledged April 14 that the bill's final form will likely exclude 'sweeping liability relief' due to opposition — a partial policy outcome that post-dates yesterday's scan. No major new events April 17-18 beyond the bill's April 24 committee deadline approaching. Florida AG investigation (Apr 9), stalking victim lawsuit/court order (Apr 10/13), and FSU shooting details (Apr 15) remain active. Composite 40.6 — within 1 point of the developing/functional band boundary at 40. The Illinois bill vote approaching April 24 is a key watch date for OpenAI's governance posture score.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.news_summary: Carrying forward from the April 17 scan: the Oxfam/MSF/Save the Children 6-month Gaza ceasefire humanitarian scorecard (April 16), OHCHR's statement that 'Palestinians across Gaza remain unsafe six months on,' and OCHA Situation Report No. 69 confirm ongoing aid blockade with all border crossings closed since late February. Only 110 trucks/day of humanitarian aid entering (vs. 500 needed). 150,000 children require acute malnutrition treatment; at least 2 children killed or injured daily since ceasefire. No significant new developments April 17-18 beyond continued coverage. However, the OCHA SitRep No. 69 (likely released this week) is expected to carry updated access figures. Assessment remains urgent given assessed April 17 and sustained high-severity humanitarian situation.

top_entities.name: Meta Platforms
top_entities.news_summary: Carrying forward the active child safety litigation cluster from yesterday's scan, with new April 16-17 developments: (1) Senators Dick Durbin and Josh Hawley reintroduced the STOP CSAM Act April 16, citing the NM and CA Meta verdicts. (2) Senator Markey called for House passage of COPPA 2.0 (passed Senate unanimously in March). (3) House Energy and Commerce Committee advanced the Kids Online Safety Act March 5. The legislative momentum triggered by the $375M New Mexico verdict (Mar 24) and CA $6M addiction verdict (Mar 25) continues to accelerate. TechPolicy.Press April 3 analysis: 'Platform Design Litigation Yields Historic Verdicts Against Meta and Google.' Legal experts state the new wave of cases is likely to proceed without Section 230 protection. The Hill: 'verdicts spur new momentum for kids online safety push.' Composite 12.2 (critical band).

top_entities.name: North Korea
top_entities.news_summary: HRW published a focused March 19, 2026 report explicitly linking North Korea's nuclear weapons program to human rights abuses — arbitrary detention, torture, forced labor, enforced disappearances — as integral mechanisms for funding and securing the weapons apparatus. UN Special Rapporteur Elizabeth Salmón told the Human Rights Council that the situation 'had showed no improvement and, in many instances, had degraded' over the past decade. The South Korean government under President Lee Jae-myung weakened North Korea human rights policies in 2025: cut radio broadcasts into North Korea, cancelled the annual report on North Korea human rights conditions. The US-based HRANA continues to document individual victims. Never assessed. Composite 0 (critical band).

top_entities.name: Ethiopia
top_entities.news_summary: OHCHR Commissioner Türk warned in February 2026 of a new Tigray crisis amid renewed fighting between rival Tigrayan factions and cross-border conflict with Afar. Al Jazeera published January 23, 2026: 'No respite in Tigray a year after US aid cuts' — 1,300+ people died in the past three years from hunger and lack of medicine. US USAID cuts have caused humanitarian organizations to suspend or scale back in Tigray; the most vulnerable now lack food, water, sanitation, and medical care. HRW World Report 2026 documents continued armed hostilities in Amhara and Oromia, forced displacement from Western Tigray, and violations by both federal and TPLF-aligned forces. Never assessed. Composite not found — requires entity confirmation from rotation state.

top_entities.name: Alphabet (Google)
top_entities.news_summary: Google's AdX antitrust remedy ruling remains overdue (Judge Brinkema's self-imposed March 31 deadline passed; now entering third week of delay). Legal analysts expect imminent release — this week or next. DOJ seeks forced divestiture of AdX and DFP; Google argues for behavioral remedies only. Separately, the D.C. Circuit is preparing to hear the DOJ/38-states Chrome/Android breakup appeal. A mass arbitration by advertisers (potentially $218B exposure) was filed April 13-14 based on the existing monopoly findings. The $6M California social media addiction verdict naming YouTube (alongside Meta) on March 25, 2026 adds an additional accountability track. Assessed April 17 — AdX ruling when it drops will be major. The April 21 week is a key watch window.

top_entities.name: xAI/Grok
top_entities.news_summary: NAACP v. xAI / MZX Tech (filed April 14, 2026) continues gaining coverage through April 16-18. The Daily Record (April 16) confirmed the filing. Key confirmed facts: 27 unpermitted methane gas turbines in Southaven, MS (majority-Black neighborhood already failing EPA smog standards); potential annual emissions of 1,700+ tons NOx (likely largest industrial NOx source in greater Memphis area), 180 tons fine particulate matter, 19 tons formaldehyde. No air permit was ever applied for. Relief sought: declare Clean Air Act violation, shutdown of unpermitted turbines, best available control technology requirement, daily financial penalties. Earthjustice and SELC are co-counsel. Composite 2.2 (critical band). Separately, NAACP has also petitioned Mississippi regulators to revoke a March 2026 permit for xAI's planned permanent expansion to 41 turbines.

top_entities.name: New York City
top_entities.news_summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani released the Preliminary Citywide Racial Equity Plan on April 6, 2026 — the first governmentwide racial equity framework in NYC's history. The plan sets data-driven agency goals across seven domains including Housing and Preservation, Community Safety/Rights/Accountability, Economy, and Health and Wellbeing. The plan aims to address systemic disparities in policing, housing displacement, and access to public services that disproportionately harm Black and Latino residents. NYC also published a True Cost of Living Measure. Never assessed. Composite 48.4 (functional band). Significance: NYC is the highest-ranked US city in the index, making it a benchmark-setting signal for other US cities.

sector_alerts.sector: Fortune 500 — Worker Safety: Amazon PDX9 Death
sector_alerts.alert: An Amazon worker died at the Troutdale, Oregon PDX9 facility on April 6, 2026. Management ordered other employees to continue loading trucks for over an hour as the body remained on the floor. Oregon OSHA's 'non-work related' classification applies only to reporting requirements, not to workplace causation. Employees cite heat conditions caused by newly installed soundproof curtains. Coverage spread April 13-16 across multiple major outlets. This is the most serious worker safety incident flagged in the current scan window and comes on top of Amazon's first-ever Teamsters bargaining order (April 3) and the NLRB Palmdale contractor settlement (April 13).

sector_alerts.sector: Countries — Active Atrocity and Repression Events
sector_alerts.alert: Three significant April 2026 country-level events not yet captured in prior assessments: (1) Russia declared Memorial 'extremist' (April 9) and its youngest political prisoner (now 17) faces new charges (April 15) — political prisoner count now 1,217+. (2) South Sudan: OHCHR issued two April 2026 emergency alerts — civilian protection crisis and child trafficking crisis driven by SSPDF-SPLA-IO fighting; UNSC UNMISS mandate expires April 30. (3) Ukraine Easter ceasefire collapsed on Day 1 (April 11-12) — both sides alleged hundreds of violations; Russia refused extension. Iran's April 13 EU sanctions renewal and continued executions of January protesters is also actively developing.

sector_alerts.sector: Healthcare — UnitedHealth Group Q1 2026 Earnings Watch (April 21)
sector_alerts.alert: UnitedHealth Group Q1 2026 earnings release is set for April 21. This will be the first earnings call since CEO Stephen Hemsley returned after Brian Thompson's murder and the DOJ criminal Medicare fraud investigation became public. Full-year 2025 earnings fell 41%. Analysts expect Q1 2026 to be a pivotal disclosure event for the DOJ probe. The prior authorization reforms pledged after the December 2024 CEO shooting are in force as of January 2026 but are narrowly scoped. Multiple health insurer CEOs faced congressional grilling in January 2026 over claims denials. The April 21 earnings call is a major watch event for compassion-relevant governance disclosure.

sector_alerts.sector: AI Labs — Illinois AI Liability Bills Committee Deadline April 24
sector_alerts.alert: The Illinois Senate committee deadline for SB 3444 (OpenAI-backed liability shield) and SB 3261 (Anthropic-backed strict safety/audit bill) is April 24, 2026. Senator Cunningham acknowledged on April 14 that SB 3444's sweeping liability relief provisions are 'highly unlikely' to survive intact, suggesting Anthropic's public opposition and the Gizmodo-covered 'OpenAI-Anthropic cold war in Illinois' may have shifted the legislative outcome. The April 24 vote outcome is a direct governance signal for both OpenAI and Anthropic's accountability posture scores.

Illinois SB 3444 committee vote April 24 is the next key date. Florida AG investigation and stalking victim court order remain active. Prior assessment April 17 may need revision after the vote outcome.
